paper_title: Isotope Enrichment Factors for Nitrite and Nitrate Reduction

methods.description: This method determines the variations in nitrogen and oxygen isotope ratios under denitrifying conditions using nitrite and nitrate as electron acceptors.

steps.description: Determine N and O isotopic enrichment factors for nitrite reduction under denitrifying conditions with NO2− as the sole electron acceptor.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate isotopic enrichment factors for nitrite reduction
outputs: N and O isotopic enrichment factors

steps.description: Use the same experimental approach with NO3− as electron acceptor to determine N (ε15N) and O (ε18O) isotope enrichment during denitrification of NO3−.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate isotopic enrichment factors for nitrate reduction
outputs: N and O isotopic enrichment factors

methods.description: This method involves incubating muddy organic sediments with nitrite or nitrate to study nitrate reduction potential and isotope variations.

steps.description: Collect muddy organic sediments from the Morbras Stream, a tributary of the Marne River.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from Morbras Stream

steps.description: Homogenize the sediment in the laboratory prior to the experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: homogenize sediment samples

steps.description: Weigh 30 grams of sediment in 300 ml glass bottles and add 280 mL of nitrite or nitrate in milliQ water to create a sediment slurry.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare sediment slurry with nitrite or nitrate

steps.description: Flush slurries with He for twenty minutes to achieve anoxic conditions before crimp capping the bottles.
steps.searchable_summary: flush sediment slurry with He to create anoxic conditions

steps.description: Incubate bottles under continuous agitation in the dark at 20 °C for specified durations for low and high nitrite/nitrate treatments.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate sediment slurry under specified conditions

steps.description: Centrifuge the slurry at 4000 rpm for 15 minutes and filter the supernatant to remove all sediment.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge and filter sediment slurry

steps.description: Preserve solutions with mercuric chloride to avoid residual transformation of dissolved compounds.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve filtered supernatant with mercuric chloride

steps.description: Determine nitrite and nitrate concentrations using colorimetry.
steps.searchable_summary: measure nitrite and nitrate concentrations by colorimetry

steps.description: Determine ammonium concentrations at the beginning and end of experiments to check the ammonium budget.
steps.searchable_summary: measure ammonium concentrations

steps.description: Freeze and store an aliquot of the solution for subsequent N and O isotope analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: store solution aliquot for isotope analysis

methods.description: This method involves determining nutrient concentrations using colorimetric methods.

steps.description: Determine nitrate concentrations using the colorimetric hydrazine method.
steps.searchable_summary: measure nitrate concentrations using hydrazine method

steps.description: Measure nitrite concentrations directly by colorimetry using sulphanilamide and N-1-naphthylethylenediamine dihydrochloride.
steps.searchable_summary: measure nitrite concentrations by colorimetry

steps.description: Determine ammonium concentrations using sodium nitroprussiate and sodium hydroxide.
steps.searchable_summary: measure ammonium concentrations using sodium nitroprussiate

methods.description: This method measures nitrogen and oxygen isotope ratios of nitrate and nitrite using a modified protocol.

steps.description: Reduce nitrate to nitrite in an activated column of cadmium.
steps.searchable_summary: reduce nitrate to nitrite using cadmium column

steps.description: Calculate yields of nitrate reduction using concentration measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate reduction yields from concentration data

steps.description: Dilute liquid samples with a salted buffer to obtain a concentration of 20 µM of NO2− at the column outlet.
steps.searchable_summary: dilute samples with salted buffer for NO2− concentration

steps.description: Inject azide solution to reduce NO2− to N2O and place vials in a water bath at 30 °C for one hour.
steps.searchable_summary: reduce NO2− to N2O using azide solution

steps.description: Measure isotope compositions of N2O samples with an isotope ratio mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure isotope ratios using IRMS
